codeReview.summary: The implementation is solid and well-structured. Three source modules (config.py, sae_loader.py, sae_inference.py) and a comprehensive test file (446 lines, 34 tests) fulfill all five validation assertions. Code follows project conventions: frozen dataclasses, TYPE_CHECKING guards for heavy imports, torch.no_grad() for inference, CPU-only tensors, full type annotations. All static analysis gates pass (ty check, ruff check, ruff format). The only notable gap is that load_base_model() does not move the model to config.device after loading (it only sets eval mode), but since the default is 'cpu' and all tests run on CPU, this is not blocking. Tests use graceful skipif for gated model access.

issues.description: load_base_model() loads the model without moving it to config.device. The docstring says '(optionally) moved to the configured device' but no model.to(config.device) call exists. Since default device is 'cpu' and HuggingFace models load on CPU by default, this works for the current use case but could surprise users specifying a non-CPU device.

extract_activations() hardcodes the layer access pattern as model.model.layers[layer], which works for Gemma-2, LLaMA, Mistral etc. However, the getattr fallback chain (layers_module.layer) may not work for all architectures. The docstring correctly documents the supported architectures, so this is acceptable for the current scope.

should_filter() silently clamps out-of-range feature indices rather than raising an error. This could mask configuration bugs. A warning log or ValueError would make debugging easier for users who accidentally specify indices beyond the SAE feature dimension.

VAL-DATA-001.evidence: test_sae_loads_without_error asserts loaded_sae is not None (SAE side). TestBaseModelLoading.test_model_loads_without_error asserts model and tokenizer are not None (base model side, skipped without HF_TOKEN as documented). load_sae() uses sae_lens.SAE.from_pretrained() which raises OSError on failure. All tests run on CPU with device='cpu'.

VAL-DATA-002.evidence: test_encode_shape_batch_seq_features creates random (2, 32, d_model) tensor, runs run_sae_inference(), and asserts feature_acts.shape == (batch, seq_len, n_sae_features). The implementation in sae_inference.py calls sae.encode(activations) with torch.no_grad() and returns the result directly.

VAL-DATA-003.evidence: test_configurable_indices_and_threshold demonstrates that different index sets produce different filtering results with the same activation tensor. FilterConfig is a frozen dataclass with configurable feature_indices (tuple[int, ...]) and threshold (float). PipelineConfig composes both configs.

VAL-DATA-004.evidence: test_exceeds_threshold_returns_true: tensor [0.1, 0.9, 0.2], indices=[1], threshold=0.5 → True. test_below_threshold_returns_false: same vector, threshold=0.95 → False. test_exact_threshold_is_not_exceeded: value 0.5 at threshold 0.5 → False (strict > comparison). Implementation uses (selected > config.threshold).any().

VAL-DATA-008.evidence: test_activation_shape_invariant_batch_size iterates batch sizes (1, 4, 8, 16) and asserts feature_acts.shape[-1] == n_sae_features for each. Additional test_batch_size_1_shape and test_single_token_shape cover edge cases. The implementation processes each batch independently through sae.encode().

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the pattern of using TYPE_CHECKING guards for heavy third-party imports (sae_lens, transformers). This is a good pattern already mentioned in AGENTS.md coding conventions indirectly (CPU-only testing, no cross-contamination). Worth keeping as-is.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: sae_loader.py lines 13-16 and sae_inference.py lines 15-18 both use TYPE_CHECKING imports for sae_lens.SAE and transformers types.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services or long-running processes were needed. The feature is pure computation (model loading + inference). services.yaml is correctly empty for services.

The worker deviated from a hypothetical 'always run all tests including gated ones' by using pytest.mark.skipif for gated model tests. This was justified and documented in skillFeedback. No skill file changes are needed since the deviation was environment-specific.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations shows: 'Marked gated-model tests with pytest.mark.skipif when HF_TOKEN is not set, rather than failing'. This is a sensible approach per the feature precondition stating 'HF_TOKEN environment variable available'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered that Gemma 2 2B is a gated model requiring HF_TOKEN. This is a known HuggingFace pattern but worth recording for future workers who may encounter similar gated models in this project.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Test file line 28-31: _skip_no_hf_token = pytest.mark.skipif(not os.environ.get('HF_TOKEN'), reason='HF_TOKEN not set; gated Gemma 2 2B model requires authentication'). Config uses model_name='google/gemma-2-2b'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The sae-lens library's SAE.encode() method returns a tensor directly (not a named tuple). Worker used this correctly. The API pattern is: sae.encode(activations) → torch.Tensor of shape (batch, seq_len, d_sae). This could be useful for future SAE-related features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: sae_inference.py line 34-35: feature_acts = sae.encode(activations). Test confirms shape (batch, seq_len, n_sae_features).

summary: The sae-loader-inference feature is well-implemented and passes all five assigned validation assertions (VAL-DATA-001, VAL-DATA-002, VAL-DATA-003, VAL-DATA-004, VAL-DATA-008). Three clean source modules with frozen dataclass configs, lazy imports, and torch.no_grad() inference. Comprehensive test suite (34 tests, 28 pass + 6 skip without HF_TOKEN). All static analysis gates pass. Three non-blocking issues noted: missing model.to(device) in load_base_model, silent index clamping in should_filter, and hardcoded layer access pattern in extract_activations. None are blocking since the current use case is CPU-only with known Gemma-2 architecture. Status: PASS.
